title: KR Passage Record
description: Schema for a single passage record — the output of the passaging engine (محرك التقطيع) consumed by the atomization engine (محرك التذرير). Each passage is a contiguous, topically coherent segment of normalized content that serves as the processing unit for extraction. One record per line in the JSONL output. Source-agnostic: operates below the normalization boundary.

passage_id.description: Sequential passage identifier within the source. Format: P001, P002, etc.

division_ids.description: IDs of the leaf division(s) this passage covers from the normalized package's division tree. Usually one; multiple if tiny siblings were merged.

title.description: Human-readable title for the passage. Derived from the primary division's title.

heading_path.description: Ancestor division titles from root to this passage's division, for hierarchical context.

start_seq_index.description: seq_index of the first page (inclusive). Machine-authoritative page reference.

volume.description: Volume number for multi-volume sources. null for single-volume.

digestible.description: Whether this passage contains content suitable for atomization. Always true for passages (non-digestible divisions are skipped).

content_type.description: Content classification. 'teaching' = explanatory content. 'exercise' = practice problems. 'mixed' = both. 'introduction' = introductory/prefatory content.

sizing_action.description: 'none' = standard-sized. 'merged' = multiple small divisions merged. 'split' = a large division was split. 'flagged_long' = >20 pages, not split, flagged for human review.

sizing_notes.description: Explanation of sizing action. null if sizing_action is 'none'.

split_method.description: How the split point was determined.

merged_division_ids.description: IDs of divisions merged into this passage.

science_id.description: Science classification for this passage. Inherited from the source's science_scope unless the source is multi_science, in which case per-section. Carries science identity through the pipeline per §7.3.

predecessor_passage_id.description: ID of the preceding passage in document order. null for the first passage.

successor_passage_id.description: ID of the following passage in document order. null for the last passage.

passage_text.description: The complete concatenated text content for this passage, assembled from the normalized pages in the passage's seq_index range. Provided directly so downstream engines (atomization, excerpting) do not need page-level access to the normalized package.

source_seq_index.description: seq_index of the page this footnote originated from.

items.description: A footnote relevant to this passage.

passage_footnotes.description: Array of all footnotes from pages within this passage's range, with source page attribution.
